questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Former mining regions are being revitalised through:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The furniture (A) / in this room (B) / are very (C) / expensive. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Each of (A) / the participants (B) / were given (C) / a certificate. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

According to the passage, India has the ____ largest coal reserves in the world.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / more smarter (B) / than his (C) / elder brother. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The Coal Mines Regulations were enacted in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The team (A) / are working (B) / on the new (C) / project since May. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Coal grading is primarily based on:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

The word 'legacy' in the passage most nearly means:

Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The furniture in this room are expensive.
options: The furniture in this room are expensive.
The furnitures in this room are expensive.
The furniture in this room is expensive.
The furniture in this room were expensive.
questions.solution: The furniture in this room is expensive.

A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.stem: A theodolite has an optical plummet for:
questions.solution: Optical plummet is used for precise centering over a point.

questions.stem: The velocity of water hammer in a rigid pipe is:
questions.solution: Water hammer wave speed = √(K/ρ) for rigid pipes; less for elastic pipes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 100 m. With back sight 1.2 m and fore sight 0.8 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lug angles in a steel tension member are provided to:
options: Reduce the length of the end connection
questions.solution: Lug angles add connecting area so the gusset connection length is shortened.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The actual size of a standard modular brick (without mortar) is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 × 90 × 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 × 100 × 100 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic mean depth of a circular pipe running full of diameter D is:
questions.solution: For a full circular pipe, hydraulic radius R = A/P = D/4.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contour lines crossing a ridge or valley are:
options: V-shaped (V points upstream for valley, downstream for ridge)
questions.solution: Contours form V-shape: apex upstream for valley, downstream for ridge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor γ_mb for bolted connections as per IS 800 (LSM) is:
questions.solution: γ_mb = 1.25 for bolts/rivets/pins; γ_mw = 1.25 for welds; γ_m0 = 1.1 for section yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gypsum plaster is also known as:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows at 2 m/s in a 0.25 m diameter pipe of length 1000 m, f = 0.025. The friction head loss is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gales' traverse table is used for:
questions.solution: Gales' table = traverse calculation of latitudes/departures and closing errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The design strength of a bolt in shear is given by:
questions.solution: Shear strength of bolt V_nsb = f_u/(√3 × γ_mb) as per IS 800 (LSM).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The softening point of bitumen (80/100 grade) is typically between:
questions.solution: 80/100 grade bitumen softening point = 40-50°C (Ring & Ball test).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a broad-crested weir, the discharge is proportional to:
questions.solution: Q ∝ Cd L H^(3/2) for broad-crested weir.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The local attraction in compass surveying is caused by:
options: Presence of magnetic materials near the compass
questions.solution: Local attraction = magnetic interference from iron ore, structures, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An angle tension member connected through one leg only suffers shear lag, which:
options: Reduces its effective strength below the gross-section yield value
Increases its strength above gross-section yield
questions.solution: Non-uniform stress transfer (shear lag) means the full section is not fully effective, lowering the usable tensile capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The defect in timber caused by natural growth is called:
questions.solution: Knot = natural growth defect. Check/split = drying defect. Shake = separation along grain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows through a pipe of diameter 0.3 m at 2 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In photogrammetry, the principal point is:
options: The focal point of the lens
The foot of the perpendicular from the perspective centre to the photo plane
questions.solution: Principal point = intersection of optical axis with photo plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plastic modulus of a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: S = bd²/4 (plastic section modulus). Z = bd²/6 (elastic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The porosity of a good building stone should be:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump occurs when flow changes from:
questions.solution: Hydraulic jump = transition from supercritical to subcritical (Fr₁ > 1 to Fr₂ < 1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flying height for a scale of 1:5000 with f = 150 mm is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a gusset plate connection, the block shear strength is checked at:
options: The end portion of the connected member
questions.solution: Block shear = failure along a plane through bolt holes at the connection end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Paints are applied to surfaces primarily for:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a floating body, stable equilibrium requires the metacentric height to be:
questions.solution: A positive metacentric height (M above G) ensures stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IRC, the desirable length of a transition curve is:
questions.solution: IRC empirical: L_s = 2.7 v²/R (v in km/h, R in m).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.7 and void ratio e = 0.5. The dry unit weight is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The preconsolidation pressure of a soil can be determined by:
questions.solution: Casagrande's graphical method on e-logσ' curve gives preconsolidation pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The digestion of sludge in a septic tank is:
questions.solution: Septic tank: anaerobic digestion of settled sludge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 101.1 m. A foresight of 1.8 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two-way slab action occurs when:
questions.solution: Two-way slab: L_y/L_x ≤ 2 (bending in both directions).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 10 m carries a central point load of 5 kN. The fixed-end moment at each support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a pipe of diameter 0.1 m and length 8 m, water flows at 1 m/s. Darcy friction factor f = 0.025. The head loss due to friction is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of permeability of a soil is 1×10⁻⁴ cm/s. This soil is best classified as:
questions.solution: Typical k: gravel > 10⁻¹, sand 10⁻³-10⁻⁴, silt 10⁻⁴-10⁻⁶, clay < 10⁻⁷ cm/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=40$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS satellites orbit at approximately:
questions.solution: GPS satellites in Medium Earth Orbit at ~20,200 km altitude.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC beam has effective depth 350 mm, Ast = 1% of bd, fy = 415 MPa, fck = 30 MPa. Its moment capacity (limit state) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the conjugate beam method, the shear in the conjugate beam represents:
questions.solution: Conjugate beam: V_conc = θ_real, M_conc = δ_real.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The depth of centre of pressure for a vertical rectangular surface of width b and depth d submerged with top edge at free surface is:
questions.solution: h_cp = 2d/3 from free surface for vertical rectangle with top edge at surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flow net consists of:
questions.solution: Flow net = orthogonal grid of flow lines (streamlines) and equipotential lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 10 m carries a point load of 15 kN at mid-span. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The per capita water demand for domestic purposes in urban India is about:
questions.solution: CPHEEO recommends 135 LPCD for domestic needs in urban areas.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 102.5 m. A foresight of 2.5 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sedimentation analysis is based on:
questions.solution: Stokes law: terminal velocity of settling sphere in a fluid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The volume of coarse aggregate per unit volume of concrete for w/c = 0.5 and 20 mm aggregate is about:
questions.solution: Table 3, IS 10262: for 20 mm agg, w/c=0.5, concrete vol = 0.62 m³ per m³ of concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.68, e = 0.8, S = 0.85. The water content is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M20 concrete, width 250 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 20 MPa, b = 250 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times20\times250\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 139.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.7, e = 1.0 and is fully saturated. The saturated unit weight is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of longitudinal bars in a circular column shall not be less than:
questions.solution: Min 6 bars for circular column, 4 for rectangular column.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a flow net, the intersection angle between flow and equipotential lines is:
questions.solution: Flow lines and equipotentials intersect at right angles (orthogonal).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In WSM, the neutral axis depth for a balanced section is:
questions.solution: k = mσ_cbc/(mσ_cbc + σ_st) for balanced section in WSM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic mean depth for a circular pipe flowing full is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.02, hydraulic radius R = 0.5 m and slope S = 0.0016, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.02, R = 0.5 m, S = 0.0016.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.02}\times0.5^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0016}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 1.26$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For stable floating bodies, the metacentre should be:
questions.solution: GM > 0 for stability → M above G.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.4 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.4^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.93$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relation between porosity n and void ratio e is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 8 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 8 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{8\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times10\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 10.7$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 3 m carries a central point load of 25 kN. The fixed-end moment at each support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum efficiency of a Pelton turbine is achieved when the bucket speed is:
questions.solution: η_max at u = v/2 (bucket speed = 0.5 × jet velocity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A retaining wall of 6 m height supports cohesionless fill with γ = 17 kN/m³ and φ = 30°. The total active thrust per unit length (Rankine) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a point load of 25 kN at 2 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Influence lines are used to determine:
options: Variation of a function (BM, SF, reaction) as a unit load moves across the structure
questions.solution: IL = response function due to moving unit load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 3 m wide carries 8 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 8 m³/s, B = 3 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{8}{3}=2.67$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{2.67^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.9$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows at 3 m/s in a 0.4 m diameter pipe of length 500 m, f = 0.02. The friction head loss is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular gate of width 1 m and depth 3 m is submerged vertically with top at water surface. The total hydrostatic force is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a hydraulic jump in an open channel is correct?
options: It converts subcritical flow to supercritical.
It can occur only in closed pipes.
It dissipates energy while changing supercritical flow to subcritical.
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump takes fast, shallow supercritical flow to slow, deep subcritical flow with a significant loss of energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 4 m under a head of 0.4 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 4 m, H = 0.4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times4\times0.4^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 1.85$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.02, hydraulic radius R = 0.5 m and slope S = 0.0009, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.02, R = 0.5 m, S = 0.0009.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.02}\times0.5^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0009}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 0.94$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: For a rectangle, maximum shear stress is 1.5 times the average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 3 m carries a point load of 10 kN at mid-span. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a point load of 25 kN at mid-span. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam under combined bending and shear, the maximum principal stress occurs at:
options: Top or bottom fibre (depending on loading)
questions.solution: Max principal stress at extreme fibre (max σ, min τ).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a point load of 25 kN at 1 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 100.4 m. A foresight of 2.0 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a closed traverse, the sum of latitudes should be:
questions.solution: Sum of latitudes = 0 for a closed traverse (project closes back to start).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modular ratio m for design of RC sections as per working stress method is:
questions.solution: m = 280/(3σ_cbc) as per IS 456 for WSM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative skin friction on a pile occurs when:
options: Soil settles more than the pile
Pile settles more than the soil
questions.solution: Negative skin friction = downdrag (soil moving down relative to pile).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The BM diagram of a simply supported beam under UDL is:
questions.solution: SSB + UDL: BM diagram = parabola (max at midspan).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which of Terzaghi's one-dimensional consolidation assumptions is stated INCORRECTLY below?
options: Soil grains and pore water are incompressible.
questions.solution: Terzaghi's theory assumes a fully saturated soil; 'partially saturated' is the false assumption.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+80\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=66.19$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's one-dimensional theory deals with the:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's theory describes one-dimensional consolidation of saturated soils.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 200 m³ treating a flow of 100 m³/h is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of soil solids is typically about:
questions.solution: The specific gravity of most soil solids is around 2.65.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The closing error in a traverse is computed as:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 3 m² with velocity 3 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = φσs/(4τbd), where φ is bar diameter and τbd is design bond stress.
